description_long: This is a classic twin-engine propeller airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a low-wing configuration. The aircraft features two engines mounted on the leading edges of the wings, each with a visible propeller. The tail section is equipped with a conventional horizontal stabilizer and twin vertical stabilizers, forming an H-tail design. The landing gear is fixed and consists of two main wheels under the wings and a smaller tail wheel. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage, with multiple windows along the sides for pilot and passenger visibility. The exterior is finished in a uniform metallic gray color, giving it a utilitarian and robust appearance.
description: A twin-engine, low-wing propeller airplane with an H-tail and fixed landing gear, finished in metallic gray. The cockpit is forward-mounted with multiple side windows.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the twin propeller engines, forward cockpit, and main landing gear under the wings.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the H-tail configuration, twin vertical stabilizers, and the tail wheel.
description_view_2: Left side view displaying the elongated fuselage, side windows, and the registration marking on the wing.
description_view_3: Front-right view emphasizing the nose, propellers, and the symmetrical wing structure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the wings, each with a front-facing propeller.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers and a conventional horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a single tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple side windows, likely multi-seat.
